title: Multi-axis Analysis of Image Manipulation Localization
core_claim: The paper introduces AUDITS, a 530K-image benchmark for studying image manipulation detection across domain shift, quality, manipulation type, and manipulation size, and reports robustness experiments on existing localization methods.
triage_rationale: This looks like a substantial benchmark-and-evaluation paper in a timely area, with clear utility for the field even if the contribution is primarily empirical. It is better suited for review than immediate advancement because the abstract promises a broad dataset and robustness study but does not yet show a novel algorithmic breakthrough.
likely_red_flags: Benchmark paper rather than a new method
Claims are evaluation-heavy and may depend on dataset construction choices
Abstract does not specify strong quantitative gains or a clear methodological novelty
